name: Hardee's Cinnamon Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
MIXTURE - Empty the box of cereal into a blender. Add cinnamon and brown sugar, then pulse on-off for about 3 seconds or until crumbled but not powdered. Transfer to a small bowl. Stir in melted butter with a fork until the mixture is completely softened. Set aside.
BISCUIT DOUGH - In a 2-quart mixing bowl, combine Bisquick, sugar, and raisins. Pour buttermilk, carbonated water, and vanilla into a separate measuring cup without stirring, then pour into the Bisquick mixture. Mix with a fork until all liquid is absorbed. Knead with hands, dipping into additional Bisquick as needed, to form a smooth dough that doesn't stick. Break dough into 4 or 5 portions in the bowl. Sprinkle crumbled mixture over the dough and work it into your hands until evenly distributed throughout the dough. Divide dough into 12 equal parts and shape each into about 1/2-inch thick patties. Arrange close together in a Pam-sprayed 2-8 inch round layer cake pans.
Bake at 400 degrees for 25 minutes or until evenly golden. Remove pan to wire rack and spread icing over the biscuits using the following recipe or your favorite cream cheese recipe.
ICING - In a small bowl with an electric mixer on high speed, beat 2 tablespoons melted butter, 1 teaspoon vanilla, 2 tablespoons sour cream, a dash of salt, and 1-1/2 cups powdered sugar until smooth. Place a rounded tablespoon of icing on top of each biscuit right out of the oven, letting it set for half a minute before spreading. Refrigerate any leftover icing in a covered container to use within 2 weeks or freeze to use in 6 months.
